This enterprise, named 'Antong Auto Parts,' was accurately categorized by officials as 'Auto Parts and Accessories Manufacturing' and rated as a 'Small Enterprise.' Tuqiao Industrial Park is a highly typical physical manufacturing and logistics distribution hub in the far suburbs of Nanjing. 50 workers and 5 million in funds are enough to support a sizable physical compound in a township industrial park. However, when analyzing its business license, the probe found that its business did not stop at simple 'processing,' but extended very pragmatically towards the back end: 'warehousing services; loading and unloading services; road freight transport.' The truth is extremely clear and vivid: this is a typical heavy-asset entity with 'Factory-Warehouse-Logistics Integration.' The boss rented or bought a large courtyard in the Tuqiao Industrial Park. The front/side of the courtyard is a workshop where dozens of workers are 'processing auto parts' on machine tools; the rear half of the courtyard is a 'warehousing' storehouse, stacking finished products or storing goods for other enterprises; there are also trucks parked in the courtyard, with dedicated drivers and loaders responsible for 'road freight transport and loading/unloading.' It doesn't engage in illusory R&D but solidly provides a one-stop heavy-labor service from hardware processing to warehousing and logistics. The system has accurately anchored its true coordinates at the bottom of the automotive supply chain.
